messages.author: MAD_Mesh
messages.text: Helo! It is great to see that someone form ES is alive You could try narrow + CR5. It will be compatible with CR8 but a bit faster. Let me know when you will be ON. We have ~25km in a straight line and some hills in the middle. So chances dor contact are low, but we could try. My main repeater+ room are live 24/7.

messages.author: calde_core

messages.content_html: Hi, just checking if this community is still running (Se habla en español o solo inglés?)

messages.author: MAD_Mesh
messages.text: Hi/hola! Yes we are alive and kicking! Coverage is from Hoyo de Manzanares up to Guadalajara, We have connected Pozuelo, Getafe, Rivas, Cobeña, Coslada, Alcala, Las Matas, Tres Cantos and more. 99% español, but you can use whatever you want. Someone will answer 30+ rpt active and new ppl are joining every week. Recently even Bot has joined our community and is helping us in testing So if you're in the area, just switch to EU Narrow and join us
messages.content_html: Hi/hola! Yes we are alive and kicking! messages.author: calde_core
messages.text: Sí, uso la preset EU narrow. Uso heltecV3 sin altímetro asique no estoy seguro, pero mi zona se marca como 625m (±5m) y estoy en un octavo piso con la antena cerca de la ventana.

messages.author: calde_core
messages.text: Es una DIYmall 868MHz, de 195mm montada en un conector de 5dBi (Aun estoy empezando, no se si necesitas más datos para saber que antena es?) He probado a llevarme el nodo de paseo por madrid, por la zona de Carabanchel, Usera y Villaverde Alto. Lo que he hecho es, por cada vez que he cambiado de lugar, mandar un Alert de 1 hop y otro Alert con repetidores. Ademástambién uso la herramienta Discover Nearby Nodes para repetidores y para sensores. También he añadido manualmente un repetidor que tengo cerca de casa y se supone que en rango y le he hecho Ping. Todo sin éxito.
messages.content_html: Es una DIYmall 868MHz, de 195mm montada en un conector de 5dBi (Aun estoy empezando, no se si necesitas más datos para saber que antena es?) messages.author: gorbypark
messages.text: Anyone know why Barcelona seems to use the "Portugal" preset? I am looking to start a repeater here in Valencia. There seems to be only one so far but it is also on the Portugal preset. There's two nodes on the meshcore map that are on EU/UK Narrow. I currently can't discover any nodes from my part of the city. The existing repeater is about 6.5km away Wondering which channel preset I should choose and if the Madrid community has any insights on the EU/UK Narrow vs Portugal presets thing (edited)
messages.content_html: Anyone know why Barcelona seems to use the "Portugal" preset? I am looking to start a repeater here in Valencia. There seems to be only one so far but it is also on the Portugal preset. There's two nodes on the meshcore map that are on EU/UK Narrow. I currently can't discover any nodes from my part of the city. The existing repeater is about 6.5km away Wondering which channel preset I should choose and if the Madrid community has any insights on the EU/UK Narrow vs Portugal presets thing (edited)

messages.author: MAD_Mesh
messages.text: Hello. The most important thing will be to use Narrow. It reduces significantly the noise and improves connectivity. It will be also "long range" if you put antena in a good place. In Madrid we are getting regular direct contact between rpt that are ~50km away on omni directional antennas with 3.5dbi. Two most distant repeaters with direct contsct are ~80km away. So I would say Narrow EU works OK. Probably will get to the discussion eu vs Portugal setup, but in my opinion, universality is very important, and the possibility of connecting with other countries in the future is crucial.
messages.content_html: Hello. The most important thing will be to use Narrow. It reduces significantly the noise and improves connectivity. It will be also "long range" if you put antena in a good place. In Madrid we are getting regular direct contact between rpt that are ~50km away on omni directional antennas with 3.5dbi. Two most distant repeaters with direct contsct are ~80km away. So I would say Narrow EU works OK. Probably will get to the discussion eu vs Portugal setup, but in my opinion, universality is very important, and the possibility of connecting with other countries in the future is crucial.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: MAD_Mesh
messages.text: Hello everyone! I am happy to announce that we managed to cross Gadarrama Mountains on the north of Madrid and connect to Segovia! First repeaters in Segovia are operating now 24/7.

messages.text: We would need to install more repeaters on roof tops of Madrid City! I could install one where I live. Just imagine, if there is another (and even longer) blackout...Madrid needs this. messages.author: dendrite
messages.text: Yes, Lavapiés, Madrid! I live on the last 4th floor with access to the roof (I live just below it.) Pointing to the south, the terrain profiles seem good, except towards Mostoles. I am planning to install a Heltec T114 V2 with Meshcore. With Yagi Antennas it would be possible to connect the center (Lavapiés) with these areas, meanwhile a second omnidirectional node could cover more from the city center. heywhatsthat.com/profiler.html was used for these profiles between two given points on Google Maps. (edited)

With Yagi Antennas it would be possible to connect the center (Lavapiés) with these areas, meanwhile a second omnidirectional node could cover more from the city center. heywhatsthat.com/profiler.html was used for these profiles between two given points on Google Maps. messages.text: Please set rpt with omni first (preset EU narrow). On the roof if possible. Yagi may not even be needed. We have ~80 rpts connected and most of the madrid and toledo covered.
